dna-category.amazonCategory: Cookbooks, Food & Wine > Cooking by Ingredient > Vegetables

dna-reader.readerDescription: Home cook, 25–45, confident enough to follow recipes but frustrated by the gap between recipe instructions and actual execution. Has a decent knife but no formal training. Wants to cook faster and waste less.
dna-reader.readerProblem: Slow, inconsistent knife work makes cooking feel laborious and creates uneven results
dna-reader.existingKnowledge: Can follow a recipe; knows a chef's knife from a paring knife; has no formal technique

dna-transform.beforeState: Treats the knife as a blunt instrument; hacks rather than cuts; loses confidence when a recipe says 'finely dice'
dna-transform.afterState: Uses a chef's knife with a stable grip and controlled motion; produces consistent cuts on demand; reduces prep time by 40%

dna-idea.bigIdea: Every knife skill that matters in a home kitchen is learnable in under 20 hours — if you practice in the right order.

dna-author.authorAngle: Culinary school instructor who has taught knife skills to 800+ adult learners. Knows exactly where beginners get stuck and what drills unlock progress fastest.

dna-promise.corePromise: Learn every knife skill you need for home cooking — in the right order, with concrete drills and clear milestones.
dna-promise.workingSubtitle: A Skill-by-Skill Guide to Confident, Fast Knife Work

comps.title: Jacques Pépin: New Complete Techniques
comps.author: Jacques Pépin
comps.gap: Reference format — not structured as a skill ladder; assumes reader selects what they need

comps.title: Salt, Fat, Acid, Heat
comps.author: Samin Nosrat
comps.gap: Covers all cooking elements; knife skills are one chapter, not the focus

dna-comps.gap: First book structured as a progressive skill ladder specifically for knife technique — not a reference, not a technique encyclopedia, but a deliberate learning sequence.

dna-evidence.uncertaintyHandling: Claim only what drills have been tested on real learners. Mark professional-standard targets vs. home-cook targets clearly.

dna-commercial.successMetric: 8,000 sales in 12 months; cookery school adoption

dna-consolidate.positioningStatement: For home cooks frustrated by slow, inconsistent prep, Knife Skills from Zero delivers a structured 20-hour skill ladder — the only knife book organised as a deliberate learning sequence rather than a technique encyclopedia.

pc-skill.targetSkill: Home kitchen knife work: grip, motion, and all essential cuts for vegetable and protein prep

pc-start-level.startingLevel: Can hold a chef's knife and make rough cuts. Has no formal grip technique. Slow and unsure on fine cuts (julienne, brunoise, chiffonade).

pc-end-state.endStateDescription: Reader can perform all standard home-kitchen cuts — slice, chop, mince, julienne, brunoise, chiffonade — with a stable grip and controlled motion, producing consistent results at a pace that doesn't slow recipe execution.
measurableCriteria: Julienne a carrot to 3mm x 3mm x 60mm in under 4 minutes
Mince a shallot without crying (correct technique prevents cell rupture)
Fabricate a whole chicken in under 10 minutes

subSkills.label: Pinch grip
subSkills.description: The foundational hold — pinch the blade between thumb and forefinger, curl remaining fingers around the handle

subSkills.label: The claw (guide hand)
subSkills.description: Curl guide-hand fingers so knuckles guide the blade and fingertips are protected

subSkills.label: Draw cut (slicing motion)
subSkills.description: Pull the knife toward you through the ingredient — most efficient for most ingredients

subSkills.label: Rocking chop
subSkills.description: Pivot on the tip; used for herbs and aromatics

subSkills.label: Round slice
subSkills.description: Uniform round slices of cylindrical vegetables; requires stable claw and consistent draw

subSkills.label: Julienne
subSkills.description: Matchstick cut: planks → sticks. Requires draw cut and consistent thickness

subSkills.label: Brunoise (fine dice)
subSkills.description: 3mm cube: planks → sticks → dice. Requires julienne as prerequisite

subSkills.label: Chiffonade
subSkills.description: Fine ribbon cut for leafy herbs and greens; requires rocking chop motion

subSkills.label: Mince (aromatics)
subSkills.description: Very fine chop for garlic, shallots, ginger; two-stage: rough chop → gather → fine chop

subSkills.label: Whole-bird fabrication
subSkills.description: Breaking down a whole chicken into parts; requires confidence with the joint
